title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, and Status Contracts · Go Semantics Case: Preconditions Conditional Methods
summary: A task service uses POST for every action, mutates state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without a resource or method contract. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo preconditions conditional methods increment in resource, method, status, and retry semantics table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.
Choose status, Location, validators, and response content for create, replace, partial update, asynchronous work, and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Preconditions Conditional Methods · Theory Preconditions Conditional Methods
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2179 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, status, and durable effect; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace.
